ops.title: ccx CLI namespaced (vcs/code/repo) + new ship/show/history/locate landed 2026-07-02; Homebrew release must ship before guard rewrites work against PATH ccx
ops.body:
Commit d7263d8b (main, 2026-07-02) namespaced the ccx CLI into `ccx vcs` (diff, ship, show, history) / `ccx code` (read, outline, search, grep, symbol, deps, related, replace) / `ccx repo` (overview, find, locate), with NO back-compat aliases (hard move, user-mandated). MCP mirror tools renamed to `ccx_vcs_diff`/`ccx_code_read`/… (hand-registered literals in internal/mcpserver/server.go — they do NOT follow the cobra tree). ship/show/history/locate are CLI-only, no MCP tools.

**Why:** the guard pack now rewrites/steers to namespaced commands (`ccx code read …`), but the released Homebrew ccx (≤0.2.1) is pre-namespace — until a new `v*` tag is pushed (goreleaser → Homebrew tap) and consumers `brew upgrade ccx`, every guard rewrite executed against the PATH binary fails with `unknown command`. Guard block messages carry native escape hatches (Read offset/limit) precisely for this window.

**How to apply:** before touching guard steer-to strings or docs, check the installed `ccx --version` vs the repo tree; when asked to release, tag on a merged main commit (release.yml refuses non-main tags). Delete this memory once the release has shipped and the plugin+formula are both upgraded.

Related: [[cc-context-architecture]], [[capt-hook-cc-context-wiring]].
